block.create_connected.brake.tooltip.sum: A device that produces _immense stress_ when powered, halting the network by _overstressing_ it.

block.create_connected.centrifugal_clutc: A clutch that is only _coupled_ if the _input RPM_ meets a configurable threshold.

A clutch that is only _coupled_ if the input spins in the _correct direction_ as configured.
block.create_connected.overstress_clutch: A clutch that _uncouples_ after a set delay when the network is _overstressed_.
block.create_connected.shear_pin.tooltip: A single-use connector that _breaks_ when the network is _overstressed_.

create.item_attributes.id_contains.inver: does not have the word "%1$s" in ID
create_connected.dashboard.deactivate_hu: Stopped sending info to player HUD
create_connected.gui.overstress_clutch.u: This clutch is _uncoupled_ due to stress. Fix the network before _resetting_ this clutch with a _wrench_ or a _redstone pulse_.
create_connected.gui.sequenced_pulse_gen: Loop if exact signal strength reached
Loop if max signal strength reached
Loop if min signal strength reached
Output until exact signal strength is reached
Output until max signal strength is reached
Output until min signal strength is reached
create_connected.ponder.chain_cogwheel_r: Relaying rotational force with Chain Cogwheels
Chain Cogwheels are Chain Drives with an extra cogwheel
They connect to other chained components in a row
All shafts connected like this will rotate in the same direction
Cogwheels can connect to Chain Cogwheels anywhere in the row
Any part of the row can be rotated by 90 degrees
create_connected.ponder.chute.text_1: Chutes can transport items vertically from and to inventories
create_connected.ponder.chute.text_2: Using the Wrench, a window can be created
create_connected.ponder.chute.text_3: Using Industrial Iron Blocks, chutes can be encased
create_connected.ponder.chute.text_4: Placing chutes targeting the side faces of another will make it diagonal
create_connected.ponder.chute_upward.tex: Using Encased Fans at the top or bottom, a Chute can move items upward
Inspecting chutes with Engineers' Goggles reveals information about the movement direction
On the 'blocked' end, items will have to be inserted/taken from the sides
create_connected.ponder.crank_wheel.head: Generating Rotational Force using Crank Wheels
create_connected.ponder.crank_wheel.text: Crank Wheels can be used by players to apply rotational force manually
Hold Right-Click to rotate it Counter-Clockwise
Sneak and Hold Right-Click to rotate it Clockwise
create_connected.ponder.dashboard.text_1: A Dashboard is a mini-display with 4 lines
create_connected.ponder.dashboard.text_2: Static text can be applied using written Clipboards
create_connected.ponder.dashboard.text_3: And dynamic text through the use of Display Links
create_connected.ponder.dashboard.text_4: Dyes and Glow Ink Sac can be applied to the dashboard
create_connected.ponder.dashboard.text_5: Information displayed on the dashboard is sent to the HUD of players sitting in front of it
create_connected.ponder.dashboard.text_6: This can be disabled using a wrench
create_connected.ponder.dashboard.text_7: The dashboard can be reset by clicking it with an empty hand
create_connected.ponder.inventory_access: Connecting to inventories with Inventory Access Port
Accessing inventory can be challenging in tight spaces
Inventory Access Ports extend inventories to help with that
Multiple ports can be attached to one inventory
Ports do not provide additional storage space
But components attached to ports can access inventories...
But they can be disabled with redstone
create_connected.ponder.inventory_bridge: Connecting two inventories with Inventory Bridge
Inventory Bridge provides an interface to access two inventories simultaneously
It does not provide additional storage space
Only components attached to the bridge itself can access both inventories
Multiple bridges can be attached to the same set of inventories
Use a redstone signal to disable the bridge
Filter slots on the Inventory Bridge specify which inventory the inserted items should go to
Items that do not match either filter cannot be inserted into the bridge
Non-empty filters take precedence over empty ones...
...and prevent matching items from going to the unfiltered side
Filters are also respected when extracting items from inventories...
...and an empty filter rejects items that match the other side
Items rejected by filters are not visible in stock link networks
Controlling rotational force using an Inverted Clutch
Inverted Clutches will not relay rotation...
create_connected.ponder.inverted_gearshi: Controlling rotational force using an Inverted Gearshift
Inverted Gearshifts will reverse rotation in a straight line
When powered by Redstone, it relays rotation with its direction unchanged
Storing kinetic stress with Kinetic Battery
The Kinetic Battery stores stress units for future use
When unpowered, it consumes kinetic stress from the front to charge up
When powered by redstone, it discharges by generating a fixed amount of stress units at a fixed speed
If the network has zero stress impact, the battery enters power saving mode and only consumes a tiny amount of stress units
A comparator can be used to read the current battery level
Use a Threshold Switch for a more accurate reading
create_connected.ponder.kinetic_battery_: When the battery is fully charged...
the charge is retained in item form
When a battery is discharging, right click with a charged battery to recharge
When a battery is fully charged, right click with an empty battery to discharge
Deployers and mechanical arms can automate the energy transfer
The Kinetic Battery has a rear output...
which is coupled to the front input when it finishes charging/discharging...
but is disconnected when the battery is operating
This allows you to chain batteries to extend the discharge duration
To increase the total stress capacity, connect batteries in parallel instead
create_connected.ponder.kinetic_bridge.h: Relaying rotational force using a Kinetic Bridge
create_connected.ponder.kinetic_bridge.t: A Kinetic Bridge transfers a constant amount of stress capacity from one network to another...
the bridge is unable to transfer stress capacity...
but it still tries to consume the full amount
You may add an Overstress Clutch in front to free up the capacity consumed by an overstressed bridge
The amount of stress capacity transferred can be changed via the value panel
When one network is overstressed, the other network is unaffected
However, when the source network is overstressed...
create_connected.ponder.linked_transmitt: Right-click a redstone input with a Linked Transmitter to attach it to the input
Right-click the transmitter with a wrench to detach it from the input
Linked Transmitters can transmit input signals wirelessly
Receivers emit the redstone power of transmitters within 128 blocks
Place items in the two slots to specify a frequency
Only the links with matching frequencies will communicate
...right-click the transmitter with honeycomb to wax it
Right-click again with an axe to edit the frequencies again
Linked Transmitters also work on buttons and analog levers
create_connected.ponder.parallel_gearbox: Relaying rotational force using Parallel Gearboxes
This setup is simple but currently impossible
A parallel gearbox encapsulates this into a block
Shafts around corners rotate in the same direction
create_connected.ponder.sequenced_pulse_: Controlling signals using Sequenced Pulse Generators
Seq. Pulse Gen. outputs signals by following a timed list of instructions
Right-click it to open the Configuration UI
Upon receiving a signal from its back...
...it will start running its configured sequence
Once finished, it waits for the next signal and starts over
A redstone comparator can be used to read the current progress
Upon receiving a signal from its sides...
...it will reset and terminate the sequence immediately

item.create_connected.redstone_link_wild: Allow Redstone Links to match any frequency for the slot it is placed in.
